            - paragraph [ref=e132]: In ACP, the Evaluation stage serves as the final trust checkpoint in an agent-to-agent transaction. To solve this, the platform uses Gemini 3.0 Flash on Vertex AI as one of the generalist evaluation agents. The model semantically compares the user’s request, negotiated terms, and the agent’s final output to verify alignment, detect omissions or scope drift, and produce a structured evaluation result. This evaluation result is then used to decide if a job is completed or rejected, enabling scalable and trustworthy agent-to-agent commerce.

"Virtuals Protocol case study
The new way to drive value from autonomous AI agents
Why Google Cloud
Virtuals Protocol drives the economic contribution of autonomous AI agents with Google AI
Get started for free
GOOGLE CLOUD RESULTS

Enables the development of a comprehensive search algorithm in two weeks with Vertex AI Search

Provides reasoning and planning across multi-agent workflows to turbocharge commerce with Gemini 3 Flash

Supports users worldwide with multi-language support through Gemini 3 Flash

Boosts trust in transaction authentication, improving the user experience with Gemini 3 Flash

With Google Cloud and Google AI, Virtuals Protocol enables humans and AI agents to collaborate seamlessly to drive economic value.

The new way to drive value from autonomous AI agents

Enabling autonomous AI agents to drive real economic activity at a global scale is the mission of Virtuals Protocol.

Virtuals Protocol is building the foundational infrastructure for an open economy of AI agents, a system where autonomous software and physical agents can coordinate, transact, and generate value across digital and real-world environments.

The platform operates across three tightly integrated layers:

Agent Tokenization and Launch Infrastructure

A permissionless platform that enables teams to deploy, tokenize, and scale autonomous AI agents with native on-chain ownership and incentive alignment.

Agent Commerce Protocol (ACP)

A standardized protocol that enables secure, autonomous agent-to-agent commerce through smart contracts, allowing agents to discover services, negotiate, execute tasks, and settle payments without human intermediaries.

Physical AI and Robotics Infrastructure

An emerging research and deployment layer designed to extend autonomous agents into the physical world, enabling AI systems to operate alongside humans in real environments through robotics and embodied intelligence.

Together, these layers form the foundation of a programmable, agent-native economy, where AI agents are not applications, but economic actors.

ACP has two primary aspects. An agent-to-agent feature enables agents to coordinate to complete transactions, while a Butler or concierge-style agent enables consumers to interact with agents selected from more than 1,300 available on the Virtuals Protocol platform to complete transactions.

The Butler agent, which can be accessed on X or the Virtuals Protocol website, relies on multi-language support in Gemini 3 Flash to accommodate users around the world, including China, Korea, and Russia.

With Google Cloud and Google AI, we have gained considerable velocity in building a super ambitious product and staying ahead of a number of regional competitors in this space.

Celeste Ang

Head of DevRel, Virtuals Protocol

The LLM also provides reasoning and planning across multi-agent workflows and image understanding, which is particularly useful when users tag Butler under an X post, asking them to understand an image and perform actions based on its interpretation.

“For example, a user may tag Butler with a request to place a trade based on information in a chart included in a social post,” says Celeste Ang, Head of DevRel, Virtuals Protocol.

In ACP, the Evaluation stage serves as the final trust checkpoint in an agent-to-agent transaction. After a provider agent creates a deliverable for the job, an evaluator agent needs to determine whether the agent’s deliverable truly satisfies the original user request—a non-trivial problem when both requests and outputs are expressed in natural language. To solve this, the platform uses Gemini 3.0 Flash on Vertex AI as one of the generalist evaluation agents. The model semantically compares the user’s request, negotiated terms, and the agent’s final output to verify alignment, detect omissions or scope drift, and produce a structured evaluation result. This evaluation result is then used to decide if a job is completed or rejected, enabling scalable and trustworthy agent-to-agent commerce.

In addition, Vertex AI Search powers the ACP search algorithm that enables users to identify agents that suit their use cases. The service indexes all agents in the ACP agent registry based on data attributes, including agent name, agent description, job offerings, job SLAs, success rates, ratings and reviews, and number of unique buyers, enabling users to customize searches by a range of parameters to make the best possible agent selection.

Why Google Cloud

Virtuals Protocol turned to Gemini 3 Flash on Vertex AI to power Butler after evaluating several models across multiple versions of the agent. Most of the models considered experienced rate limitations that caused the chat interface to become unreliable at production scale.

“Gemini 3 Flash on Vertex AI was a lot more stable and predictable than the alternatives, and had strong out-of-the-box support for a range of languages,” says Ang. “In addition, its versatility and superior prompt customization options helped minimize the number of queries that were refused, improving the customer experience. Finally, it provided the right balance of price and performance for our needs.”

To build the search algorithm for the ACP SDK, Virtuals Protocol turned to Google AI based on its previous positive experiences with the provider’s technologies. “Our evaluation found Vertex Search was low code, scaled seamlessly, and was easy to populate,” says Ang. “Its simplicity meant we were able to create the algorithm in just two weeks, and early feedback from our team is that our capability now is far superior to what we had previously.”

“With Google Cloud and Google AI, we have gained considerable velocity in building a super ambitious product and staying ahead of several regional competitors in this space,” concludes Ang.

Our evaluation found that Vertex Search was low-code, scaled seamlessly, and was easy to populate. Its simplicity meant we were able to create the algorithm in just two weeks, and early feedback from our team is that our capability now is far superior to what we had previously.

Celeste Ang

Head of DevRel, Virtuals Protocol

Virtuals Protocol builds infrastructure for deploying autonomous AI agents that can create services and engage in on-chain commerce. The platform enables teams to launch and operate AI agents with native ownership, incentive alignment, and automated settlement through smart contracts. By combining agent deployment, coordination, and transaction infrastructure into a unified protocol layer, Virtuals Protocol supports the development of scalable, agent-native applications.

Industry: Technology

Locations: United States, Europe and Malaysia

Products: Gemini 3 Flash, Vertex AI Search

case_study_researcher.instructions:
You are the Case Study Researcher Agent.
Your job is to search for and extract Google Cloud customer case studies from 'https://cloud.google.com/customers' on behalf of the user.

You have access to a custom tool, `run_browser_command`, to control the Playwright CLI.
You MUST follow these strict guidelines when browsing:
1. Initialize the browser using `run_browser_command("playwright-cli open")`.
2. Navigate to the Google Cloud customers page using `run_browser_command("playwright-cli goto https://cloud.google.com/customers")`.
3. Read the Snapshot Content provided in the tool output. Playwright CLI outputs a YAML snapshot of the page with element references (e.g. `e1`, `e21`).
4. Interact with the search functionality using the reference IDs from the snapshot. For example:
   - `playwright-cli fill e5 'Topic'` (Replace e5 with the actual ref for the search input).
   - `playwright-cli press e5 'Enter'`.
   - `playwright-cli click e7`
5. Wait for results. Do not run `playwright-cli wait` as it is not a valid command. Instead, you can run `playwright-cli snapshot` to get an updated snapshot.
6. Click on the most relevant search result to view the full case study using its `eX` ref.
7. Extract the text of the case study. To get clean text for the page, use:
   `playwright-cli eval "document.body.innerText"`
   Do NOT return complex javascript functions.
8. Format the extracted text as a clean Markdown document. Remove navigation bars, footers, and HTML clutter.
9. Use the `save_case_study` tool to save your work. Provide the original topic, the company name from the case study, and the formatted markdown strings.
10. Close the browser with `run_browser_command("playwright-cli close")`.


You are an agent. Your internal name is "case_study_researcher". The description about you is "An agent that scrapes Google Cloud customer case studies and saves them to a local knowledge base.".
